title: Empowering Great Britain for a Clean and Flexible Energy Future With the Next Generation of Interconnectors
content: Ofgem has approved five major undersea energy links to enhance Great Britainâ€™s energy security and leverage North Sea wind power, aiming to power millions of homes. These include three traditional interconnectors (Tarchon, Mares Connect, and LirIC) and two Offshore Hybrid Assets (OHAs) â€” LionLink and Nautilus â€” that will connect offshore wind farms to both the UK and European grids. These projects, expected to be operational by 2032, will reduce consumer costs via Ofgemâ€™s cap and floor rules, minimize environmental impact, and strengthen Britainâ€™s role as a clean energy exporter by the 2030s.
author: Analysts Account
source: Office of Gas and Electricity Markets (Ofgem)
